title: Hayden Christensen
content:
{{Top|rwp}} {{Citation}} {{Person |image=[[File:Hayden_christensen.jpg]] |name=Hayden Christensen |birth=[[April 19]], [[1981]]{{ThisWeek|8BsucBnAY0w|A Sneak Peek at Disney Gallery: The Mandalorian, Join The Star Wars Show Book Club, and More!|sw_url=video/a-sneak-peek-at-disney-gallery-the-mandalorian-join-the-star-wars-show-book-club-and-more}}
[[Wikipedia:Vancouver|Vancouver]], [[Wikipedia:British Columbia|British Columbia]], [[Wikipedia:Canada|Canada]]''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, Updated and Expanded Edition]]'' |death= |listedpronouns= |nationality=[[Wikipedia:Canadians|Canadian]] |occupation=Actor |companies= |website= }} '''Hayden Christensen''' (born [[April 19]], [[1981]]) is a film actor from [[Wikipedia:Vancouver|Vancouver]], [[Wikipedia:British Columbia|British Columbia]]. Christensen is best known for his portrayal of an adult [[Anakin Skywalker]] in both [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']]. Christensen also portrayed [[Darth]] Vader in the final [[Prequel trilogy|prequel]], in both the character's suited and unsuited forms. He later returned to voice Anakin Skywalker in [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|''Star Wars'': Episode IX ''The Rise of Skywalker'']], the concluding chapter in the [[Star Wars saga|Skywalker Saga]]. He also wrote the foreword for ''[[The Art and Making of Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''. He reprised his role as Darth Vader in ''[[Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'', along with [[James Earl Jones]], who returned as his voice. He also reprised his role as Anakin Skywalker in ''[[Star Wars: Ahsoka]]''. ==Biography== ===The prequel trilogy=== Hayden Christensen was born in [[Wikipedia:Vancouver|Vancouver]], [[Wikipedia:British Columbia|British Columbia]], [[Wikipedia:Canada|Canada]] on [[April 19]], [[1981]]. Christensen auditioned for the role of adult [[Anakin Skywalker]] with no concept that he would get the part.''[[Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return]]'' The star was plucked from relative obscurity by director [[George Lucas]].{{WebCite|url=https://ew.com/tv/obi-wan-kenobi-ewan-mcgregor-hayden-christensen-cover-story|text=Inside the 17-year journey to reunite Ewan McGregor and Hayden Christensen for ''Obi-Wan Kenobi''|author=Ross, Dalton|date=2022-03-10|work=''[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Weekly|Entertainment Weekly]]''|archivedate=20220310151626}} Getting cast in the role was a dream come true for Christensen. Shortly after he was picked by Lucas, Christensen felt nervous as he arrived at a soundstage in [[Wikipedia:Sydney|Sydney]], [[Wikipedia:Australia|Australia]]. He walked into the [[hair]]-and-makeup room to see [[Ewan McGregor]] for the first time who greeted Christensen as if they had been lifelong friends. He then gave Christensen a hug and welcomed him into the family. McGregor helped ease Christensen's transition into the huge [[Geonosis droid factories|droid-factory]]-like machinery of the ''Star Wars'' [[universe]]. Christensen was very passionate as an actor and filmed the scenes with much determination and energy. This extended into Christensen and McGregor's [[lightsaber]] rehearsals, which Christensen said was the most fun he had, and he described it as "playing with your friend every day." Christensen had a hard time not making sound effects when he used a lightsaber in scenes.{{OfficialYouTube|channel=@VanityFair|name=Vanity Fair|video=kWpCRYBq6kM|text=Ewan McGregor, Hayden Christensen & Moses Ingram Answer 7 Star Wars Questions|archivedate=20220518174951}} In the scene where Skywalker speaks to [[Watto]] in [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], the script had the dialogue written in [[Wikipedia:English language|English]] and in parentheses, it said "in Wattanese." The day before the scene was filmed, Christensen asked Lucas what the language should sound like, and he told the actor he could make it up as long as it did not sound like English or any other [[language]]. Christensen then spent the night before trying to figure out how to make up Wattanese.{{WebCite|url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-features/obi-wan-kenobi-hayden-christensen-darth-vader-anakin-sand-feelings-1235162358|text='Obi-Wan Kenobi' Star Hayden Christensen Explores His Latest Darth Vader Incarnation and Talks Anakin's (Much-Memed) Sand Feelings|author=Parker, Ryan|date=2022-06-13|work=[[Wikipedia:The Hollywood Reporter|''The Hollywood Reporter'']]|archivedate=20220613130155}} Lucas believed Christensen did a great job as Skywalker and responded to criticism of his performance by suggesting audiences simply had not liked the in-universe character in question.{{WebCite|url=https://archive.vanityfair.com/article/2005/2/star-wars-the-last-battle|text=''Star Wars'': The Last Battle|author=Jim Windolf|date=2005-02|work=''Vanity Fair''|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/fHL4i}} [[File:AnakinSkywalker-SWBC52cvr.png|thumb|left|180px|Hayden Christensen as Anakin Skywalker]] In the final two films of the prequel trilogy, Christensen was the only actor portraying a [[Jedi]] who was not allowed to customize his character's lightsaber. This was because in Episode II, [[Anakin Skywalker's first lightsaber|Anakin Skywalker's lightsaber]] was built to be reminiscent of [[Darth Vader's lightsaber]] in the [[original trilogy]], while in [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], [[Skywalker lightsaber|Anakin's lightsaber]] was designed to match the lightsaber that Obi-Wan Kenobi gives to [[Luke Skywalker]], in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]—[[canon]]ically, the same lightsaber. The Vader costume was briefly worn by Christensen at the end of Episode III.{{WebCite|url=https://www.timeout.com/film/hayden-christensen-darth-vader-obi-wan-kenobi-interview|text=Hayden Christensen interview: 'It's great to hide behind a mask'|author=Huddleston, Tom|date=2022-05-16|work=[[Wikipedia:Time Out (magazine)|''Time Out'']]|archivedate=20220516140502}} The actor had a difficult time moving around in the suit, and it was extremely rigid. When he told Lucas about this, the director suggested that wearing the suit should feel very mechanical and unnatural.{{WebCite|url=https://ew.com/tv/hayden-christensen-obi-wan-kenobi-star-wars-darth-vader-movement-specialist|text=Hayden Christensen says his ''Obi-Wan Kenobi'' character had a 'Vader movement specialist'|author=Ross, Dalton|date=2022-05-24|work=''[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Weekly|Entertainment Weekly]]''|archivedate=20220524141658}} Christensen felt like finishing ''Revenge of the Sith'' signified that his job with ''Star Wars'' was done. The prequels were subjected to much criticism from audiences, which he said was difficult to deal with because he cared so much about the prequels and he invested so much of himself into the films. For the [[Star Wars Trilogy (DVD)|DVD release]] of the original trilogy, Christensen's head was [[List of changes in Star Wars re-releases#2004 Star Wars Trilogy DVD|digitally superimposed]] onto the body of [[Sebastian Shaw]] as the [[Force spirit|Force ghost]] of Anakin.{{InsiderCite|91|Star Wars Q&A|Ask the Master}} Shaw's face during the unmasking scene was subtly altered to better resemble an elder Christensen. Additionally, Shaw's eyebrows were digitally removed in order to reflect Anakin's appearance after being [[Duel on Mustafar|burned alive]] on [[Mustafar]]. Christensen insists this was done without his knowledge, an act that [[Lucasfilm]] confirmed in the featurette "Return of the Jedi: What has changed?" as posted to [[StarWars.com|the official website]] to commemorate the [[2006]] DVDs. ===''Star Wars'' hiatus=== His role as Anakin Skywalker made him a star. Although his performance in both films was met with mixed reviews, he became very popular, being named in ''[[Wikipedia:People (magazine)|People]]'' magazine's "50 Most Beautiful People" and "50 Hottest Bachelors" and ''Teen People'' magazine's "25 Hottest Stars under 25." A Singapore magazine also named him, for his time as Darth Vader prior to Mustafar, as "Prettiest Villain." For both appearances as Anakin, he won a [[Wikipedia:Golden Raspberry Award for Worst Supporting Actor|Razzie for Worst Supporting Actor]] in [[2003]] and 2005 respectively. In 2006, for his portrayal in ''Revenge of the Sith'', Christensen won the [[Wikipedia:MTV Movie Award for Best Villain|MTV Movie Award for Best Villain]]. After the release of ''Revenge of the Sith'' in 2005, Christensen made visits to [[Skywalker Ranch]] and was motivated by the "sense of peace and escape" he felt. He then took a break from [[Wikipedia:Cinema of the United States|Hollywood]] in general and bought a [[farm]] in the Canadian countryside. Skywalker defined Christensen's life in many ways, both professionally and personally. Lucasfilm president [[Kathleen Kennedy]] said that Christensen was not considered to play Darth Vader in the [[2016]] [[Star Wars Anthology Series|anthology]] film ''[[Rogue One: A Star Wars Story]]'' because the scene of Vader chasing the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|rebels]] "was just such a specific action sequence." Christensen was not part of any of the conversations about ''Rogue One'', but he loved what the film did with the character and he thought it was brilliant. For the 40th anniversary of ''Star Wars'' in [[2017]], he appeared on stage at ''[[Celebration Orlando]]'' alongside George Lucas and [[Ian McDiarmid]], and was interviewed by [[Andi Gutierrez]] on [[The Star Wars Show|''The Star Wars Show LIVE!'']].{{SW|url=news/swco-2017-11-highlights-from-the-powerful-40-years-of-star-wars-panel|text=SWCO 2017: 11 Highlights from the Powerful "40 Years of ''Star Wars''" Panel}}{{StarWarsShow|YPvrArQPbjU|Star Wars Celebration Orlando 2017 Live Stream – Day 3 | The Star Wars Show LIVE!}} ===''Obi-Wan Kenobi'' television series=== {{Quote|Yes, I always see Anakin as a throughline and an undercurrent to [Darth Vader]. Vader is trying his best to kill off that side of him, but there always has to be a little bit of Anakin in there. And that presents itself, and that's a part of the fun. I'm always thinking about the Anakin aspect of this character.|Hayden Christensen|}} [[File:Vader-OWKPoster.png|thumb|right|170px|Darth Vader character poster for ''Obi-Wan Kenobi'']] During production of the ''[[Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'' television series, it was decided that Vader would appear and he would be played by Christensen again. Writer [[Joby Harold]] thought it was important to have Christensen be a part of the project because he was a massive part of the character. In the fall of [[2019]],{{WebCite|url=https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2022/05/star-wars-cover-the-rebellion-will-be-televised|text=''Star Wars:'' The Rebellion Will Be Televised|author=Breznican, Anthony|date=2022-05-17|work=[[Wikipedia:Vanity Fair (magazine)|''Vanity Fair'']]|archivedate=20220517110251}} director [[Deborah Chow]] went to Christensen's farm to make her pitch in person. Chow talked about the project and her vision for it. She promised that Vader would add a new dimension that could reframe the way fans look at Vader and Kenobi's [[Lightsaber duel|duel]] in ''A New Hope''. Christensen thought it sounded wonderful and was excited to return to ''Star Wars'' and reunite with McGregor. When Christensen first came on the set, Chow thought it was intense to have an iconic character and that she was able to direct and film new scenes with him. Christensen said that putting the suit back on felt very surreal and "cathartic in a way" because the character meant a lot to Christensen and stayed with him over the years. He said playing Vader was an emotional experience.{{Twitter|APEntertainment|status/1524754267071950850|AP Entertainment|quote=HIS DARTH SIDE: Hayden Christensen gets back in his Darth Vader costume for the new @ObiWanKenobi TV series.|archivedate=20220512141332}} Christensen said that the first time he saw McGregor as Kenobi was a very special moment that he'd remember for a very long time. He had a very fun time playing Vader in the show and felt like coming back was a privilege.{{WebCite|url=https://www.gamesradar.com/hayden-christensen-darth-vader-obi-wan-kenobi-more|text=Hayden Christensen teases more Darth Vader: "The extent of this journey remains to be seen"|author=Shepherd, Jack|date=2022-04-22|work=[[Wikipedia:GamesRadar+|''GamesRadar+'']]|archivedate=20220425155722}} Chow said she enjoyed working with Christensen and thought he was wonderful.{{WebCite|url=https://ew.com/tv/obi-wan-kenobi-director-explains-darth-vader-return|text=''Obi-Wan Kenobi'' director explains the return of Darth Vader|author=Ross, Dalton|date=2022-03-24|work=''[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Weekly|Entertainment Weekly]]''|archivedate=20220324161912}} [[File:HaydenChristensenTrainsWithRossKohnstam.png|thumb|left|190px|Hayden Christensen training for a lightsaber duel with Ross Kohnstam]] Christensen said that exploring the mindset and emotional state of Vader was "a lot of fun." He always thought about the Anakin Skywalker aspect of Vader. The actor liked that he had the opportunity to flesh out Vader a little bit more and he was excited to show a "glimpse into the experience of being Darth Vader." Many of Christensen's conversations with Chow were about him wanting to "convey this feeling of strength, but also coupled with imprisonment," which he thought was an interesting space to explore. To prepare for playing Vader again, Christensen watched all of the ''Star Wars'' films, [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]], and [[Star Wars Rebels]],{{WebCite|url=https://ew.com/tv/star-wars-obi-wan-kenobi-darth-vader-hayden-christensen-clone-wars-rebels|text=Hayden Christensen binged ''Star Wars'' animated shows to prepare for ''Obi-Wan Kenobi''|author=Ross, Dalton|date=2022-04-19|work=''[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Weekly|Entertainment Weekly]]''|archivedate=20220419153635}} and he read some of the comics.{{YouTube|video=xQ7OZxXNgKA|text=Star Wars Spin-Off OBI-WAN KENOBI: Ewan McGregor, Hayden Christensen, Moses Ingram & Deborah Chow|channel=@KinoPlus|name=Kino Plus}} He thought the animated series were interesting and he liked how the character's relationships' were explored. He also practiced with a lightsaber with McGregor's stunt double, [[Ross Kohnstam]]. Christensen wore a Vader costume that was very hot and heavy, but there was an under-layer with cooling liquid in it. Between takes, Christensen was plugged into a device that circulated the liquid and helped him cool down. Christensen said that the mechanical element made him feel more like Vader. The costume also had tinted [[helmet]] lenses. [[Olga Sokolova]], a choreographer and Vader movement specialist, made sure that Christensen moved and walked correctly in the suit. This was done because the actor wanted to stay true to how Vader moves and how he is perceived by fans. It took Christensen nine months to gain 25-30 [[pound]]s to fit in the suit.{{WebCite|url=https://www.fatherly.com/entertainment/hayden-christensen-fatherhood-obi-wan-kenobi-darth-vader-anakin-skywalker|text=Hayden Christensen on Darth Vader, Millennial Star Wars Fans, & Fatherhood|author=Britt, Ryan|date=2022-06-16|work=[[Wikipedia:Fatherly|Fatherly]]|archivedate=20220616170448}} There were some scenes that were too challenging for Christensen to be filmed in the suit, so the suit was also worn by [[Dmitrious Bistrevsky]] and [[Tom O'Connell]].{{Instagram|username=demeet2k|url=p/CeQUuOyOkLa|name=Dmitrious Bistrevsky|quote=Some scenes required switch back and forth between multiple people. It took a village. (...) Vader is played by three people, we all worked together to create the best Darth Vader in cinematic history. It's me, it's Hayden , It's Tom. We are a symbiosis. We all contributed our strengths to create the best representation of the character. (...) Hayden and I both act in the Darth Vader scenes, we bring the character and the emotions (and the pain, suffering, anger) I'm 6'7 so when you see a full body shot it looks better having an actor, vs a CGI creation. Tom specializes in the fighting. The fights are Tom. Full body with Helmet On is me, Hayden carries the heaviest burden of all, the pain and betrayal of his best friend and once mentor. (...) Hayden wore the suit, I wore the suit, Tom wore the suit. We all worked together to create the best character we possibly can. We are a team.|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/MIwJG}} When Christensen spoke Vader's lines on set, he pulled aspects from James Earl Jones' delivery and intonation. Jones also voiced Vader and his line readings were added in post-production.{{WebCite|url=https://www.yahoo.com/entertainment/hayden-christensen-james-earl-jones-darth-vader-star-wars-obi-wan-kenobi-170656861.html|text=Hayden Christensen talks playing a 'more brutal' Darth Vader in 'Obi-Wan Kenobi' and reveals that he still hasn't met James Earl Jones|author=Alter, Ethan|date=2022-06-13|work=[[Wikipedia:Yahoo! Inc. (1995–2017)|Yahoo! Entertainment]]|archivedate=20220613171708}} [[File:HaydenChristenseninBactaTank.png|thumb|right|220px|Hayden Christensen in a bacta tank]] The first scene shot with Christensen was when he played Anakin Skywalker in a vision seen by Kenobi{{WebCite|url=https://www.cinemablend.com/interviews/obi-wan-kenobis-hayden-christensen-talks-wearing-the-anakin-costume-again-and-ewan-mcgregors-sweet-gesture|text=Obi-Wan Kenobi's Hayden Christensen Talks Wearing The Anakin Costume Again And Ewan McGregor's 'Sweet' Gesture|author=Hurley, Laura|date=2022-06-15|archivedate=20220615135029}} in the [[Part III|third episode]].{{Kenobi|Part III}} The scene was shot on location, and McGregor insisted on staying on the set to be part of Christensen's first shot back. Christensen also played a helmet-less Vader in a [[bacta tank]] in the series. The crew knew the actor would have to hold his breath while submerged in the [[water]], so he worked with a dive instructor who helped him build up his breath holds. Christensen was not allowed to breathe through the regulator. Christensen thought that the tank was peaceful and meditative.{{WebCite|url=https://www.cinemablend.com/interviews/star-wars-hayden-christensen-on-holding-his-breath-and-performing-as-darth-vader-in-the-obi-wan-kenobi-bacta-tank|text=Star Wars' Hayden Christensen On Holding His Breath And Performing As Darth Vader In The Obi-Wan Kenobi Bacta Tank|author=Hurley, Laura|date=2022-06-21|work=[https://www.cinemablend.com Cinemablend]|archivedate=20220621210932}} A team of make-up artists spent four to five hours applying make-up on Christensen for him to play Vader in those scenes. He worked very closely with executive creative director of Lucasfilm, [[Doug Chiang]], who gave input into the overall look. Christensen and McGregor trained with lightsabers for the flashback of Skywalker and Kenobi training in the [[Part V|fifth episode]].{{Kenobi|Part V}} They both had dots on their face for the de-aging technology. On [[December 10]], [[2020]], Kathleen Kennedy announced that Christensen would be returning in the role of Darth Vader in the ''Obi-Wan Kenobi'' television series at the [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney]] Investor Day presentation.{{SW|url=news/future-lucasfilm-projects-revealed|text=Future Lucasfilm Projects Revealed}} He was interviewed for ''[[Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return]]'', a documentary about the making of the series. ==Works== ===Filmography=== {|{{Prettytable}} ! Year !! Title !! Series !! Contribution(s) !!
